_meta.description: Indicative coverage status for quantum-related scenarios under typical cyber insurance policies. Based on review of public quantum exclusion language.
sources: Lloyd's Market Bulletin Y5258 — Quantum Computer Exclusion Clause (2023) (https://assets.lloyds.com/assets/pdf-market-bulletin-y5258/1/pdf-market-bulletin-y5258.pdf)
Munich Re — Cyber Insurance & Quantum Risk (https://www.munichre.com/en/solutions/reinsurance-property-casualty/cyber-reinsurance.html)
Marsh — Quantum Risk & Insurance Gap Analysis 2023

coverageValues.covered: Typically covered under standard cyber policy language
coverageValues.excluded: Explicitly excluded by quantum/nation-state clauses or war exclusions
coverageValues.ambiguous: Coverage unclear — policy wording does not specifically address quantum-origin attacks; subject to dispute at claims time

_meta.note: Educational model — actual coverage depends on specific policy wording. Consult a licensed insurance professional.

scenarios.label: Harvest Now, Decrypt Later
scenarios.description: Retroactive exposure of data harvested today, decrypted once CRQCs are available.

scenarios.label: Digital Signature Forgery
scenarios.description: Forged certificates or code-signing via broken RSA/ECDSA.

scenarios.label: Authentication Bypass
scenarios.description: Broken public-key authentication enables unauthorized access.

scenarios.label: Supply Chain Key Compromise
scenarios.description: Compromised software signing keys enable malicious package distribution.
